commentaries.author: Clement Of Rome
commentaries.content: Truly his will shall be fulfilled swiftly and suddenly, as the Scripture testifies. - "1 Clement 23"

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For the LORD will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose Israel, and set them in their own land: and the strangers shall be joined with them, and they shall cling to the house of Jacob. Babylon was taken one hundred and seventy-two years after. (Calmet) Yet this time is counted short, compared with the monarchy, which had lasted a thousand years. (Worthington) Ground. Cyrus restored the Jews; yet all did not return at that time. Converts, Esther viii. All Idumea received circumcision under Hyrcan.

commentaries.author: Justin Martyr
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For the LORD will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose Israel, and set them in their own land: and the strangers shall be joined with them, and they shall cling to the house of Jacob. [Jewish] proselytes have no need of a new covenant, since, as one and the same law binds all who are circumcised, Scripture speaks of them as follows: “And the stranger also shall be joined to them and shall adhere to the house of Jacob.
